payload.title: Add runtime identity binding primitive
payload.summary: Operator surfaces need a reusable primitive that separates durable identity authority from volatile substrate handles and projection consumers.
payload.proposal:
# Runtime Identity Binding Primitive

Status: proposed upstream to Narada proper; direct inbox submission was blocked
because the WSL authority embodiment failed to attach its VHDX in this session.

## Proposal

Narada should admit a reusable operator-surface primitive for runtime identity
bindings.

The primitive separates:

- durable identity authority, such as a User Site identity registry;
- volatile substrate handles, such as Windows HWNDs owned by a PC Site runtime;
- projection consumers, such as overlays, bars, terminal portals, browser
  profiles, MCP clients, or session inspectors.

For the Windows overlay case, the coherent projection is:

```text
HWND -> identity_name -> label
```

The User Site owns `identity_name -> label`. The PC Site owns `HWND ->
identity_name` because HWND values are machine-local runtime facts.

Carrier facts such as visible title, terminal profile, class name, process id,
and tab title may be recorded as evidence, but they are not naming authority.
Unknown windows remain unlabeled.

## Evidence

- Title-based matching failed when visible Windows Terminal titles became
  dynamic, for example `Andrey | ~ | Context ...` and `narada`.
- Overlay `inspect` now reports `no_runtime_binding` for visible but unbound
  terminal windows.
- After explicit focused-window assertion, overlay `inspect` resolved HWND
  `4130710` to `narada-andrey.architect` through `runtime_hwnd_binding`.
evidence: Title-based matching failed when visible Windows Terminal titles became dynamic.
The User Site now models HWND -> identity_name -> label as a PC-local runtime projection.
Unknown windows remain unlabeled and inspect reports no_runtime_binding.
payload.requested_handling: Admit as Narada proper product/doctrine work if aligned, because this primitive applies beyond the Windows overlay.
